content.assessment: Structural delay of tracking has the largest potential fiscal-incidence effect among the proposals reviewed here because it removes the age-10/12 selection point that currently triggers the capitation-loss mechanism for feeder schools entirely, rather than adjusting it (F-live-education_finance-05). This is a high-magnitude intervention: converting a two-track early-selection system into a unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase would require substantial transition-phase investment (staffing, facility reallocation, curriculum harmonization) precisely in a system already ranked 41/42 in OECD education-expenditure share and 32/34 in lower-secondary instructional hours (F-live-education_finance-06), raising serious affordability and value-for-money questions absent dedicated new resources. The proposal's own phasing safeguard (multi-cohort transition) is a reasonable value-for-money risk mitigant against the kind of capacity shock documented in Poland's reversal, but the finance evidence base supplied does not include transition-cost estimates, so magnitude cannot be assessed with confidence.
opportunities: A successful comprehensive restructuring could permanently eliminate the documented capitation/peer-effect loss mechanism for feeder schools, a durable fiscal-incidence gain unavailable to admission-rule-only reforms
strengths: Removes the structural funding-loss trigger at the age-10/12 selection point entirely rather than mitigating its effects, which is the most direct fiscal-incidence fix among the proposals for the capitation mechanism identified in F-live-education_finance-05
threats: In a system ranked near the bottom of OECD/partner spending comparisons, any underfunded or rushed structural transition risks compounding existing resource scarcity (F-live-education_finance-06); the finance lens has no evidence to confirm whether adequate transition funding would be allocated
weaknesses: Requires major transition investment in a fiscal environment already characterized by low overall spending share and instructional-time scarcity (F-live-education_finance-06), and no cost estimate for this transition is available in the supplied evidence
Affordability risk compounds with implementation risk (documented under other lenses) in a resource-constrained system
